2606.15778.title: DYNA : Dynamic Episodic Memory Networks for Augmenting Large Language Models with Temporal Knowledge Graphs in Continuous Learning
2606.15778.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) struggle to incorporate new knowledge without forgetting or costly retraining. We propose DYNA, a lightweight framework that augments a frozen LLM with a temporal knowledge graph where events are nodes and temporal relations are directed, timestamped edges. The graph serves as an external, updatable memory. At query time, DYNA retrieves relevant nodes via random walks and centrality measures, then augments the LLM's response. Evaluated on three temporal recall tasks, DYNA reduces catastrophic forgetting by ~7% compared to fine-tuning and improves temporal ordering by ~5% over standard RAG. Higher graph clustering coefficients correlate with better retrieval, showing that graph structure matters. Contributions: (1) episodic memory as temporal KG, (2) retraining-free LLM augmentation, (3) graph properties as predictors of retrieval performance.
2606.15778.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 在不遺忘或高成本重訓的情況下，難以融入新知識。<lb/>我們提出了 DYNA，一個輕量級框架，通過一個時間知識圖來增強一個凍結的 LLM，其中事件為節點，時間關係為有向、帶時間戳的邊。<lb/>該圖作為一個外部的、可更新的記憶體。<lb/>在查詢時，DYNA 通過隨機漫步和中心性度量檢索相關節點，然後增強 LLM 的回應。<lb/>在三個時間回憶任務上的評估中，DYNA 對比微調減少了約 7% 的災難性遺忘，並且在時間排序上比標準 RAG 提高了約 5%。<lb/>更高的圖聚類係數與更好的檢索相關，顯示圖結構的重要性。<lb/>貢獻：(1) 作為時間 KG 的情節記憶，(2) 無需重訓的 LLM 增強，(3) 圖屬性作為檢索性能的預測因子。
2606.15778.author: Ali Sarabadani et.al.
